title: rubus hirtus var. hercynicus
intro: Rubus hirtus var. hercynicus, also known as the European dewberry, is a type of wild raspberry native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, wounds, and inflammation. The plant's berries and roots are rich in antioxidants and contain anthocyanins, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Rubus hirtus var. hercynicus can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall well-being.
